edge: The approach focuses on improving continual test-time adaptation in a memory-efficient manner by minimizing memory consumption.
Long-term adaptation often leads to catastrophic forgetting, which hinders applying TTA in real-world deployments.
The approach includes lightweight meta networks that can adapt the frozen original networks to the target domain, minimizing memory consumption.
The novel self-distilled regularization controls the output of the meta networks not to deviate significantly from the output of the frozen original networks, preserving well-trained knowledge.
Without additional memory, the regularization prevents error accumulation and catastrophic forgetting, ensuring stable performance in long-term test-time adaptation.

edge: Memory usage is a key concept when deploying models on a device, as it directly impacts the performance and efficiency of the models.
TinyTL is a seminal work in on-device learning, showcasing the importance of activation size over learnable parameters in memory bottlenecks during training.
On-device learning studies aim to decrease the size of intermediate activations, targeting fine-tuning tasks to optimize memory efficiency.
Previous TTA studies have focused on reducing learnable parameters instead of activation sizes, displaying a difference in approach compared to recent on-device learning studies.
The proposed method reduces high activation sizes required for TTA tasks, enhancing adaptation performance and improving memory efficiency.
The simple yet effective approach updates meta networks in the target domain while regularizing them with knowledge distilled from the original network, promoting memory efficiency.
A memory-efficient architecture prevents error accumulation and catastrophic forgetting commonly observed in long-term adaptation, ensuring stability and performance in the model.
